intro: After a simulation run, this page compares the observed cat-behavior distribution against Hirsch et al. (2025)'s 227 hours of direct observation. A composite score of 80 or higher means the scenario closely matches the empirical baseline.
needsRun: No simulation has run yet. Go to Settings and press Run first.
noIssues: No significant deviations; the distribution shape matches the empirical observation.
behaviorCompare: Nine-state behavior distribution (Hirsch Figure 3 vs simulation)

out_of_lounge.too_high: Cats hide in the cat room too often. Lower per-cat customer load or add more in-lounge perches.
out_of_lounge.too_low: Cats almost never retreat to the cat room. Confirm the cat-only room is available as a private space.

resting.too_high: Too much resting. Try raising arrival rate or lowering catRestProbability.
resting.too_low: Cats barely rest; unrealistic for house cats. Raise catRestProbability or extend catRestDuration.

socializing.too_high: Socializing exceeds Hirsch's 12.8%. Reduce customer-initiated interaction probability or widen personality variance.
socializing.too_low: Not enough socializing; customer experience may suffer. Raise density or extend catInteractionTime.

hidden.too_high: Hiding is excessive: cats are stressed. Reduce customer count or add more perch / cat-room space.
hidden.too_low: Cats barely hide (Hirsch reports ~10.7%); check ALERT and shelf-preference logic.

alert.too_high: Alert share is high; crowding is distressing the cats. Reduce density or speed up exits.
alert.too_low: Alert share is too low; simulation isn't capturing stress signals. Confirm occupancy modifiers apply.

grooming.too_high: Grooming exceeds Hirsch's 4.5%; GROOMING dwell time may be too long.
grooming.too_low: Grooming is suppressed, a stress signal. Try lowering customer density.

moving.too_high: Moving share too high; dwell time may be too short.
moving.too_low: Cats barely move; check activity_level distribution.

exploring.too_high: Exploring exceeds the 0.8% baseline; cats are too restless.
exploring.too_low: Exploring is suppressed, indicating a stressful environment. Lower occupancy or enrich the space.

playing.too_high: Playing exceeds Hirsch's 0.3%; this is rare, check personality vectors.
playing.too_low: Playing is near zero, which matches Hirsch, but a strict zero may mean the PLAYING branch never fires.

floor.too_high: Floor share is high; usually means there aren't enough perches or furniture spots.
floor.too_low: Cats almost never on the floor; you may have overestimated occupancy.

furniture.too_low: Furniture is ignored; review furniture spot placement.

shelf.too_high: Shelf share is high, reflecting stress under crowding (Hirsch 2025 Figure 4b).
shelf.too_low: Shelf share is low; space enrichment may be inadequate.
